proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_056 = 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_056 = 0.
Patients with feature_042 = 1 have longer mean pfs_months than patients with feature_042 = 0.
Patients with feature_048 = 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_048 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: mean PFS 3.62 (n=14969) on feature_056 vs 5.16 (n=35031) off; difference -1.54 months, t=-66.62, p~0.
Welch t-test: mean PFS 5.41 (n=17428) on feature_042 vs 4.31 (n=32572) off; difference +1.10 months, t=43.54, p~0.
Welch t-test: mean PFS 3.74 (n=5090) on feature_048 vs 4.79 (n=44910) off; difference -1.05 months, t=-29.42, p=1.4e-178.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_015 = 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_015 = 0.
Patients with feature_111 = 1 have longer mean pfs_months than patients with feature_111 = 0.
Patients with feature_040 = 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_040 = 0.
Patients with feature_039 = 1 have longer mean pfs_months than patients with feature_039 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_015 = 1 (n=17365) mean 4.32 vs feature_015 = 0 (n=32635) mean 4.88; diff -0.56 months, p=6.1e-144.
feature_111 = 1 (n=34886) mean 4.86 vs feature_111 = 0 (n=15114) mean 4.30; diff +0.57 months, p=3.7e-143.
feature_040 = 1 (n=9060) mean 4.31 vs feature_040 = 0 (n=40940) mean 4.77; diff -0.46 months, p=6.1e-70.
feature_039 = 1 (n=31954) mean 4.82 vs feature_039 = 0 (n=18046) mean 4.46; diff +0.36 months, p=5.8e-59.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_080 (continuous, range 30-90, plausibly age) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
Higher feature_063 (ordinal, levels 0/1/2) is negatively associated with pfs_months (likely a staging-like factor).
Higher feature_067 (continuous, 0-24.6) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
Higher feature_019 (continuous, 1.5-5.5) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
Higher feature_101 (continuous, 1-100) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.7015, p~0; feature_080 explains ~49% of variance in PFS by itself.
Pearson r=-0.3204, p~0. Mean PFS by level: 0->5.64, 1->4.44, 2->3.29 (approximately linear ~-1.18/level).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment for the strongest univariate predictors (feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_015, feature_111, feature_040, feature_039, feature_034, feature_080, feature_063, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101), most main effects retain significance with the same sign.

analyses.code: import statsmodels.api as sm; sm.OLS(y, sm.add_constant(df[key_cols])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS R^2=0.792. Adjusted coefficients (all p<0.001 unless noted): feature_080 +0.176, feature_063 -1.172, feature_056 -1.549, feature_042 +1.102, feature_048 -0.983, feature_015 -0.582, feature_111 +0.531, feature_040 -0.471, feature_019 +0.476, feature_067 -0.077, feature_101 -0.019. feature_039 (+0.014, p=0.32) and feature_034 (+0.002, p=0.88) lost significance after adjustment for the others, suggesting their univariate effects were largely confounded by feature_042 and feature_111.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_011 (race/ethnicity: white, asian, black, hispanic, other).
Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_089 (insurance: medicaid, private, medicare, uninsured).

analyses.result_summary: One-way ANOVA F=0.85, p=0.49. Means: white 4.679, asian 4.735, black 4.717, hispanic 4.707, other 4.762. No race differences in PFS.
One-way ANOVA F=0.31, p=0.82. Means: medicaid 4.706, private 4.685, medicare 4.692, uninsured 4.733. No insurance differences in PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of feature_042 (binary, treatment-like) varies by feature_011 (race/ethnicity); minority patients may receive feature_042 less often.
Receipt of feature_042 varies by feature_089 (insurance); patients with public/no insurance may receive feature_042 less often.

analyses.result_summary: Receipt rates: white 0.348, asian 0.345, black 0.351, hispanic 0.351, other 0.337. All within ~1.5 percentage points; chi-square not significant. No race-based access disparity.
Receipt rates: medicaid 0.349, medicare 0.350, private 0.348, uninsured 0.346. Differences <0.5 percentage points; not significant. No insurance-based access disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_042 on pfs_months differs across categories of feature_011 (treatment efficacy disparity by race).
The effect of feature_042 on pfs_months differs across categories of feature_089 (treatment efficacy disparity by insurance).

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_111 = 1 (where feature_042 has its effect), per-race differences in feature_042 effect: white +1.58, asian +1.49, hispanic +1.58, black +1.58, other +1.56. All interaction p-values >0.65. No racial disparity in treatment effect.

feature_042 receipt rate within feature_111 = 1: medicaid 0.350, medicare 0.354, private 0.345, uninsured 0.354. Effect sizes within each insurance group are equivalent. No insurance-based heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_042 on pfs_months is modified by feature_111: feature_042 produces a larger increase in PFS in feature_111 = 1 patients than in feature_111 = 0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Cell means: (111=0,042=0)=4.30; (111=0,042=1)=4.29; (111=1,042=0)=4.31; (111=1,042=1)=5.89. Interaction coefficient +1.59 (p=9.3e-229). feature_042 yields no benefit in feature_111 = 0 patients but +1.58 months in feature_111 = 1 patients. feature_111 itself has no effect among feature_042 = 0 patients (mean 4.30 vs 4.31), so feature_111 is a purely *predictive* biomarker of feature_042 efficacy, not a prognostic factor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_042 on pfs_months is reduced or abolished in patients with feature_015 = 1 (feature_015 acts as a resistance marker).
The effect of feature_042 on pfs_months is reduced or abolished in patients with feature_040 = 1 (feature_040 acts as a resistance marker).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient -1.62, p=4.0e-255. Among feature_015 = 0 patients feature_042 effect is approximately +1.6 months; among feature_015 = 1 patients the effect collapses to near zero. feature_015 is a resistance marker for feature_042.
Interaction coefficient -1.36, p=2.2e-118. feature_042 benefit is essentially eliminated in patients with feature_040 = 1. feature_040 is also a resistance marker for feature_042.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_042 produces a clinically meaningful PFS benefit only in the joint subgroup with feature_111 = 1 AND feature_015 = 0 AND feature_040 = 0; in all other patients its effect is null.

analyses.result_summary: Eligible subgroup (n=18,681; ~37% of cohort): feature_042 off mean 4.30 vs on 7.21, diff +2.91 months. Non-eligible (n=31,319): off 4.32 vs on 4.32, diff +0.003 (null). Stratification by all 8 combinations of feature_111/feature_015/feature_040 shows feature_042 effect is concentrated in (111=1, 015=0, 040=0) and is null in every other cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039 acts as a third effect modifier of feature_042 efficacy beyond feature_111/015/040.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_111 = 1 AND feature_042 = 1: feature_039 = 0 mean 5.78, feature_039 = 1 mean 5.91 (diff +0.13). Within feature_111 = 0 cells, feature_039 yields 0.04 difference whether feature_042 is given or not. The marginal main effect of feature_039 (+0.36) is largely confounded by its co-occurrence with the responsive subgroup; once feature_111 and feature_042 are controlled, feature_039 has only a small residual effect (~0.13) in responders. feature_039 is not a strong independent predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_056 on pfs_months is amplified at higher values of feature_080 (older patients lose more PFS when feature_056 is present).
The negative effect of feature_063 on pfs_months is amplified at higher values of feature_080.
The effect of feature_042 (in the responsive subgroup) is constant across feature_080 deciles (no age-based heterogeneity of treatment effect).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient -0.0112 per unit of feature_080, p=3.1e-12. Effect of feature_056 grows from ~-0.9 at feature_080=30 to ~-1.9 at feature_080=90.
Interaction coefficient -0.0057 per unit, p<1e-7. Per-level decrement of feature_063 is more severe at higher feature_080.

Within eligible subgroup, feature_042 effect by feature_080 decile ranges 2.65-3.17 months with no monotonic trend. Treatment effect is essentially constant across age. Interaction p in full model with eligible*feature_080*feature_042 is non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 0-4 ordinal features feature_021, feature_024, feature_127, feature_001, feature_073 (likely symptom or performance-status grades) are associated with pfs_months.
The 0-10 ordinal feature_016 is associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r values: feature_021 +0.0042 (p=0.34), feature_024 +0.0002 (p=0.96), feature_127 +0.0046 (p=0.30), feature_001 -0.0039 (p=0.38), feature_073 -0.0008 (p=0.86). Mean PFS varies by <0.06 months across all five levels for each feature. None of these ordinal severity-like features carry signal for PFS.
Pearson r=-0.0016, p=0.72. No signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_111 has prognostic value (predicts PFS) independent of feature_042 administration.
Among patients not receiving feature_042, feature_015, feature_040 retain prognostic significance for PFS.

analyses.result_summary: Among feature_042 = 0 patients (n=32,572): feature_111 = 0 mean 4.303, feature_111 = 1 mean 4.313, diff +0.011 months (NS). feature_111 has essentially zero prognostic value on its own; its entire marginal effect was attributable to its role as a predictive biomarker for feature_042.

Among feature_042 = 0 patients: feature_015 coef ~-0.04 (small, NS, p~0.1), feature_040 coef ~-0.02 (NS). Like feature_111, much of the marginal effect of feature_015 and feature_040 was driven by their role as resistance modifiers of feature_042 (cells where feature_042 fails to add benefit), not independent prognosis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_048, feature_019, feature_067, and feature_101 retain large independent prognostic effects on PFS even when controlling for the predictive eligible*feature_042 interaction.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + feature_056 + feature_048 + feature_019 + feature_067 + feature_101 + eligible*feature_042', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.876. Coefficients (all p<1e-50): feature_080 +0.176/unit, feature_063 -1.172/level, feature_056 -1.552, feature_048 -0.969, feature_019 +0.482/unit, feature_067 -0.077/unit, feature_101 -0.019/unit. Main effects of eligible (-0.001) and feature_042 (+0.003) collapse to zero, while the eligible:feature_042 interaction is +2.91 (p~0). Confirms feature_042 has effectively no prognostic value outside the responsive subgroup, while the rest of the predictors are prognostic regardless of treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive eligibility subgroup (feature_111 = 1, feature_015 = 0, feature_040 = 0), receipt of feature_042 differs by feature_011 (race) — i.e., minority patients are under-treated even within eligibility.
Within the eligibility subgroup, receipt of feature_042 differs by feature_089 (insurance).

analyses.result_summary: Among eligible patients only: white 0.349, asian 0.348, hispanic 0.350, black 0.349, other 0.348. Differences <0.5 percentage points; chi-square NS. No racial disparity in feature_042 access among eligible patients.
Among eligible patients: medicaid 0.349, medicare 0.354, private 0.345, uninsured 0.354. Differences ~1 percentage point; not significant. No insurance disparity in access among eligible patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_034 (binary) has a small positive marginal main effect on PFS but is non-significant after multivariable adjustment.
feature_077 has a small positive marginal main effect on PFS.

analyses.result_summary: feature_034 = 1 mean 4.76 vs 0 mean 4.65; diff +0.11 months, p=1e-6. After adjustment in the multivariable model (h13), coef +0.002, p=0.88 — confounded by feature_111/feature_042.
feature_077 = 1 mean 4.73 vs 0 mean 4.66; diff +0.066 months, p=4.9e-3. Tiny effect; likely a weak confound.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_063 acts approximately linearly (per-level decrement of ~1.18 months in PFS).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_063 level: 0 -> 5.640, 1 -> 4.444, 2 -> 3.289. Differences level0->level1: -1.20; level1->level2: -1.16. Linear approximation -1.18/level fits very well. Treating feature_063 as continuous in the multivariable model is appropriate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080 acts linearly on PFS rather than via thresholds; PFS rises smoothly across the feature_080 distribution.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_080 decile climbs monotonically from 2.4 (lowest decile, feature_080~30-50) to 7.5 (highest decile, feature_080~85-90). Coefficient ~+0.176 per unit fits the deciles well; no threshold effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within highly negative prognostic strata (feature_056 = 1 OR feature_048 = 1), feature_042 still produces a benefit in eligible patients.

analyses.result_summary: Among patients with feature_056=1 or feature_048=1: eligible+treated mean ~5.5 vs eligible+untreated ~2.7 (diff ~+2.8 months). Non-eligible stay ~2.6-2.8 regardless of treatment. The eligibility-defined treatment benefit is preserved even in the worst-prognosis strata; it stacks additively on top of poor baseline.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (race) and feature_089 (insurance) are conditionally independent of pfs_months given the prognostic and predictive features identified above.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + feature_056 + feature_048 + feature_019 + feature_067 + feature_101 + eligible*feature_042 + C(feature_011) + C(feature_089)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After full adjustment, all C(feature_011) and C(feature_089) coefficients are tiny (|coef| < 0.04 months) and non-significant (p > 0.3 for every level). Race and insurance contribute essentially nothing to PFS once the clinical features are accounted for.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080 mean does not differ by feature_011 (race) — no demographic confounding of the strong feature_080 effect through race.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_080 by race: white 65.0, asian 64.9, black 65.0, hispanic 65.0, other 64.9. ANOVA p>0.4. Race groups balance on feature_080.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_111, feature_015, feature_040 are roughly independent (their joint prevalence is well-approximated by the product of marginals — no co-clustering).

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['feature_111'], df['feature_015']) and similar for feature_040; chi-square test of independence
analyses.result_summary: Pairwise cross-tabs show approximate independence: feature_111 prevalence among feature_015=0 is 0.698 vs feature_015=1 is 0.696 (chi-square p>0.5). feature_111 vs feature_040: 0.698 vs 0.695. feature_015 vs feature_040: 0.351 vs 0.351. The three predictive features mark genuinely different biological factors rather than a single subtype.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pairwise treatment-by-treatment interactions among the strongest binary features (feature_042, feature_111, feature_015, feature_040, feature_039) are dominated by feature_042 interactions; non-feature_042 interactions are smaller.

analyses.code: loop over pairs and fit smf.ols('pfs_months ~ a * b', data=df) for each
analyses.result_summary: Top binary x binary interactions ranked by p-value all involve feature_042 as one of the partners or feature_111 (its biomarker): feature_042 x feature_015 -1.62 (p=4e-255); feature_042 x feature_111 +1.59 (p=9e-229); feature_042 x feature_040 -1.36 (p=2e-118); feature_042 x feature_039 +1.09 (p=2e-117); feature_111 x feature_040 -0.66 (p=4e-26 — explained by feature_042 x 111 x 040 triple interaction); feature_015 x feature_040 +0.70 (p=4e-31, NS after adjustment for feature_042 triple). Without feature_042 in the picture, feature_015 and feature_040 add additively but with no further interaction. The cohort's heterogeneity is concentrated in the predictive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious final model — pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + feature_056 + feature_048 + feature_019 + feature_067 + feature_101 + eligible*feature_042 — captures most explainable variance in PFS, and adding race/insurance/other binary features yields negligible R^2 gain.

analyses.code: compare R^2 of final model vs same+race+insurance+all top 20 binaries
analyses.result_summary: Parsimonious model R^2 = 0.876. Adding feature_011 (race) + feature_089 (insurance) raises R^2 by 0.0001. Adding the next 10 binary features (feature_034, feature_077, feature_086, ...) raises R^2 to 0.879 (a ~0.003 gain — small but not zero). The bulk of explainable PFS variance is captured by 7 prognostic features plus the predictive feature_042 x eligibility interaction.
